scope_summary: ISS-1734 (severity=moderate, status=assigned-analyst, age 17d at this run) tracks SEC-4.2.2 c3 H2 "operational-rule framing incomplete." The curmudgeon (SEC-4.2.2.c3.json, reviewed 2026-04-28T14:52:37Z) recommended adding a single sentence acknowledging the predictions.html Tier 1 coherence criterion ("No coherent signal at ≥3 stations with r > 0.5 on verified Kp<2 day") and showing why it does not rescue the misstatement. The recommended paragraph was authored as EXP-275 (created 2026-04-28T17:25:32Z) and integrated by the decider 2026-04-29T01:18:46Z (tracker.items[EXP-275].integrated_at; the integration shipped via workspace sync 68722ed3, 02:04:44Z). The integrated paragraph is verbatim present in current data/sections.json part4.html at character index 42,400 (verified 2026-05-16 this run on FUSE): "The same predictions.html document also publishes a separate Tier 1 falsification criterion — \"No coherent signal at ≥3 stations with r > 0.5 on verified Kp<2 day\" — but that rule does not rescue the misstatement either. It still treats the globe as predicting a null pattern (the same Tier 1 section of predictions.html publishing the rule also publishes \"Globe prediction: 0.0 nT systematic\"), and BOU 2017's −10.9 nT signal already exhibited Chapman-pattern coherence with the August 2017 eclipse contact times..." (HTML entities preserved in source). ISS-1734 was created at 2026-04-29T09:23:45Z — more than 8 hours AFTER the patch shipped to git. The decider's issue-creation pass on the c3 review did not cross-check against the post-c3 integration state; same stale-issue-from-creation pathology as ISS-1258 (closed by EXP-403 this dispatch cycle).
kernel_of_truth: The c3 H2 curmudgeon hole was substantive at the time it was written (2026-04-28T14:52). The then-current §4.2.2 text really did frame the operational falsification rule as "tests the measured anomaly against 0 nT" without acknowledging the parallel predictions.html Tier 1 coherence criterion ("No coherent signal at ≥3 stations with r > 0.5 on verified Kp<2 day"). A sophisticated dome defender could retreat to that alternative rule and claim it is "the operational rule" with live.html and the countdown banner as simplifications. The hole was real and the curmudgeon recommendation was correct. The fix was applied within hours via EXP-275: a single inserted paragraph after §4.2.2 paragraph 5 acknowledging the predictions.html Tier 1 rule and showing why it does not rescue the misstatement (the rule still publishes "Globe prediction: 0.0 nT systematic" in the same Tier 1 block, and BOU 2017 already exhibited the ≥3-station coherent Chapman-pattern the rule asks the 2026 measurement to lack). The EXP-275 paragraph adopts the curmudgeon's suggested wording almost verbatim, with one small embellishment: it cites the 207-observation INTERMAGNET multi-station ensemble (39 eclipses including 2017) as documentation that the ≥3-station threshold was already satisfied — strengthening the curmudgeon's point that BOU 2017 satisfied the Tier 1 rule. ISS-1734 captures the same hole the curmudgeon flagged but was filed AFTER the fix shipped, and never got re-routed.
holes_addressed: SEC-4.2.2.c3 H2 (moderate): "INCOMPLETE OPERATIONAL-RULE FRAMING — the integrated text states 'tests the measured anomaly against 0 nT' but predictions.html publishes a different Tier 1 criterion (coherence/correlation, not amplitude). A sophisticated dome defender could retreat to that alternative rule." — closed by EXP-275 (integrated 2026-04-29T01:18:46Z by decider, present verbatim in part4.html at char idx 42,400). Verified 2026-05-16 this run.

anticipated_objections.objection: The 207-observation INTERMAGNET ensemble citation in the integrated paragraph goes beyond what the curmudgeon strictly recommended. A future curmudgeon could press: "you embellished the original recommendation; is the ensemble citation defensible?"
anticipated_objections.response: The ensemble citation is already in the immediately preceding paragraph of §4.2.2 (the multi-station ensemble of 207 observations across 39 total eclipses cited above the integration point), so the EXP-275 paragraph is referencing existing-and-cited material, not introducing a new claim. The strengthening is appropriate because it shows the predictions.html ≥3-station threshold was already empirically satisfied by 2017 data — which is a tighter version of the curmudgeon's implicit argument that BOU 2017 already exhibited "Chapman-pattern coherence with the 2017 eclipse contact times." The embellishment makes the kill more concrete (citing the actual N-stations satisfying the threshold) without changing the logical structure of the curmudgeon's recommended sentence.

anticipated_objections.objection: Closing ISS-1734 as OBE leaves the stale-issue-from-creation pathology unaddressed. The decider's issue-creation pass should have caught that EXP-275 had already shipped before ISS-1734 was filed. If we don't flag this structurally, the pattern keeps recurring.
anticipated_objections.response: EXP-403 (WIN-062 carve-out, ISS-1258 close-already-fixed-by-source) earlier this same dispatch cycle already documented this pathology and proposed the structural fix lives in tinker (decider issue-creation pass should cross-check against the post-review integration state before filing). Two same-cycle close-already-fixed entries within hours (ISS-1258 at 16:03, ISS-1734 now) confirm the pattern is recurring and worth a tinker proposal. The pattern: c3-or-later curmudgeon reviews can fire on text that the analyst has ALREADY drafted a fix for, the decider issue-creation pass treats the c3 review as authoritative without re-checking, and the issue lands stale-from-creation. Recommended tinker work: add a decider issue-creation pre-flight that scans expansion-tracker.json for "integrated_at" entries on the same WIN/section in the 24h window before the curmudgeon review.reviewed_at — if found, suppress the issue or downgrade to verification-class. Not in scope for this EXP; flagged for tinker via the next attention-inbox / proposal cycle.

anticipated_objections.objection: The curmudgeon's recommendation suggested insertion "after paragraph 5 (or as a parenthetical inside the BOU-2017 trilemma paragraph)." The integrated text is a standalone paragraph between paragraph 5 and the trilemma — not exactly either of the curmudgeon's two suggested placements. Is the placement defensible?
anticipated_objections.response: The standalone-paragraph placement is the cleaner of the curmudgeon's two options. The "parenthetical inside the trilemma" option would have made the trilemma paragraph denser and harder to read; the standalone-paragraph option keeps the trilemma's three-horn structure intact while introducing the alternative rule before the reader reaches the trilemma. The transition sentence ("The BOU-2017 trilemma below collapses to the same essential admission whichever rule the dome elects to defend") explicitly bridges the new paragraph to the existing trilemma. This is a stronger rhetorical structure than either of the curmudgeon's suggestions and is consistent with the curmudgeon's recommendation as "Add a single sentence acknowledging the predictions.html Tier 1 coherence rule and collapsing the retreat" (the form of the addition is left open).
